The simulated full cell entropy variation curve was determined from the same electrode balancing given in (a) and (b). Marking of corresponding ∂ O C V / ∂ SoC and EHC features according to [45]. Caption: OCV vs. temperature trajectories for both cell types at three SoCs obtained from entropy measurements. (a–c) Results for Type A cells with error bars indicating inter-cell variation between A-1 and A-3. (d–f) Results for Type B cells. Yellow markers indicate temperature step as given in Fig. 4. Dashed lines show linear regression over all temperature steps for charging (light blue) and discharging (dark blue) direction with average entropy variation given alongside. (For interpretation of the references to color in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the web version of this article.)

Entropy variation results obtained from measured OCV vs. temperatures trajectories shown in Fig. 6. (a) EHC-SoC trajectories in charging and discharging direction for Type A cells. Enclosed areas contain all EHC values, which can be determined from the different temperatures steps. Dashed lines compare two specific temperature steps as given in Fig. 4. (b) Results for B-type cells.

Observed temperature-path-dependency for both cell types. (a) Normalized voltage trajectory for four temperature steps at 30 % SoC approached in charging direction (Type A). (b) Resulting OCV vs. temperature plot over all temperature steps. Arrowheads indicate consecutive temperature steps with the enclosed area indicating the observed dependency. (c-d) Results for B-type cells at 30 % SoC approached in discharging direction.

Additional potentiometric entropy variation tests for the characterization of the observed temperature-path-dependency. All measurements were conducted on specimens A-1, A-6 and A-9 at 30 % SoC approached in charging direction. (a) Full temperature cycle between 5 °C and 55 °C for cell A-1. The order of the temperature steps is indicated over the data points. (b) Repeated partial temperature cycles between 5 °C and 25 °C. Cell A-1 experienced two full cycles, cells A-6 and A-9 experienced five full cycles.

Entropy variation and differential OCV curves obtained from aged specimen A-4 to A-9. (a, c) Entropy variation for the 15 to 25 °C temperature step for aged cells, pristine counterpart and reconstruction using half cell values and electrode balancing. (b, (d) ∂ O C V / ∂ SoC curves for the same cells including reconstruction. Line markers indicate shift of C-2 peak between A-1-3 and A-6 (b) i.e. A-9 (d).
